strengths: Nearly 20 years of hands-on engineering experience (2006–present) spanning web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and low-latency infrastructure — comfortably exceeding the 15-year minimum

Entrepreneurial builder credentials: founded and still runs GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used in production by Green Map, plus recent AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic-workflow frameworks) at re:cinq

Demonstrated technical-leadership and evangelism track record: conference talks (FOSS4G 2019), multi-day courses, full-day workshops, and internal practice-building (Refactoring Dojos, TDD/craftsmanship advocacy) — matching the role's 'inspire others through sharing insights' mandate
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and AI experience is recent (2025) and tooling-focused rather than a recognized, product-scale AI track record
Career progression tops out at Staff/Senior IC roles at small-to-mid companies — no evidence of director-level scope, strategic advisory to large customers, or influence across a large organization
Cloud expertise is applied (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) rather than platform-strategy level; current employment gap since October 2025

key_factors.factor: Depth and breadth of engineering experience (19+ years, full stack to embedded)
Founder/builder profile with shipped open-source platform and hands-on AI-agent tooling
Proven ability to teach, evangelize, and lead technical practice adoption

response.justification: This candidate clears the role's hard requirements with room to spare: a technical Bachelor's degree and nearly two decades of software engineering across radically different domains, from million-request-per-second low-latency microservices in DLang to AWS-based pricing platforms in TypeScript. What elevates the profile toward the preferred criteria is the builder-founder dimension — he created and operates GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform with real institutional users, and his most recent role involved building MCP code generators and agentic-AI frameworks, placing him hands-on at the exact frontier this role targets. Combined with a sustained record of public talks, workshops, and craftsmanship evangelism, he fits the mold of a code-fluent, entrepreneurial technologist who can prototype strategic AI opportunities and inspire teams to pursue them.
